messages.text:
**New Drive** (also referred to as the "Drive Refresh" or the "Test Drive" workstream) is a major strategic initiative to rebuild Google Drive from the ground up for the agentic era. 

Instead of acting as a passive storage bin, the goal is to transform Drive into an active, intelligent workspace. The initial target is an opt-in, standalone MVP tailored to AI-savvy SMBs, allowing the team to bypass legacy technical debt and move with high velocity [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc).

---

## Key Product Vision & "Aha" Moments
The core user experience of New Drive centers around a synthesized dashboard where Drive proactively organizes tasks and allows for instant delegation to AI agents [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc). 
* **The Omnibar:** A unified search-forward homepage that seamlessly combines classic file search with an interactive "AI Mode" [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc).
* **Test Drive Workstream:** To keep core workflows stable for Drive's 1.4 billion user base, the team established "Test Drive" as an independent, public test environment for high-risk, high-conviction experimental features [📄 Drive Refresh Meeting Notes](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1UR5cZ02YzE8JRJoE5urj1lDy3N1Liv6RRz3sioV1Cks).

---

## Strategic Roadmap & Feature Stack Rank
The team has outlined a structured, four-phase sprint plan to build out and prototype these features [📄 New Drive Feature Stack Rank](https://drive.google.com/open?id=17rPqF52FpRM57VbYwgEeTzBS-DOz2RGpgPgYwEo_4WA) [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc):

### Day 0: High-Value Basic UX Improvements
Focuses on modernization and friction reduction:
* **Toggleable Folder Navigation:** A tri-view toggle supporting:
  * *Tree View:* In-line folder expansion (up to 5 levels) with a right-hand preview column.
  * *Column View:* Up to 3 levels of hierarchy displayed side-by-side.
  * *Gallery View:* A horizontal thumbnail row showing visual file previews.
* **Universal Embedded View ("Projector"):** An embedded canvas to preview and interact with any file type—including Workspace Docs, PDFs, and videos—without opening new tabs.
* **Modern Toolbar:** Features an integrated Search bar, Gemini companion panel ("Gemini Sparkle"), Comment feed, History, and File Details.
* **Pinned Folders:** A dedicated left-navigation area where users can drag and drop up to 10 frequently used folders.

### Day 1: Hosting Third-Party (3P) AI Artifacts
Positions Drive as the premier place to run and host mini-applications (such as interactive HTML or React apps generated by AI):
* **Dynamic Artifact Rendering:** Automatically detects folders containing `.html`, `.tsx`, or `.jsx` files and renders them as live, interactive web apps inside the preview pane.
* **DOM-Aware UI Comments:** Users can drop visual comment pins directly onto specific UI elements (like buttons or forms) of the live application.
* **Session-Level History:** Clicking on a past session in the history panel updates the preview pane to render that historical state of the application.
* **State Persistence (KV Store):** Stores state data in a standard `state.json` file inside the folder so the application maintains state across local environments and the web.

### Day 2: Managing and Controlling Agents
Creating natural surfaces to collaborate with AI agents:
* delegating tasks directly to active agents in comments and the Gemini side panel.
* Live status tracking and execution progress logs for active agent tasks.
* A prioritized, auto-synthesized to-do list compiled from meeting notes, comments, and emails.

### Day 3: Creating First-Party (1P) AI Artifacts
* Dynamic, collaborative creation of complex artifacts from a blank slate based on feedback loops across meeting notes, chat, and other Workspace surfaces.

---

## Technical & Infrastructure Strategy
To deliver on this ambitious product vision, the engineering team is evaluating a modern tech stack decoupled from legacy systems [📄 A New Drive Agenda](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1wqb23Y6f8bioTgAVN1_9rDx_S0hTOqIBaVB1we_nHE8) [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc):
* **Underlying Architecture:** Testing a new setup leveraging a Client-Only Wiz frontend, Powertrain (PT), and Cello, following the **Rivian architecture proposal** [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc).
* **Code Autonomy:** A development goal has been proposed to have roughly 80%+ of the prototype's codebase written by AI coding agents [📄 A New Drive Agenda](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1wqb23Y6f8bioTgAVN1_9rDx_S0hTOqIBaVB1we_nHE8).
* **Sandboxing:** Isolating executing JavaScript from the host app’s auth tokens by rendering previews in sandboxed `iframes` [📄 New Drive Feature Stack Rank](https://drive.google.com/open?id=17rPqF52FpRM57VbYwgEeTzBS-DOz2RGpgPgYwEo_4WA).

---

## Key Upcoming Milestones
* **July 7 – 10, 2026: NYC Hackathon:** An intensive, in-person sprint in NYC where cross-functional leadership, UX, product, and engineering teams will build a functional prototype using real user data [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc). 
* **July 10, 2026: Initial Leadership Review:** Presenting the hackathon prototype to David Wurtz, Yi Zhang, and Juyun Song [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc).
* **July 16, 2026: Broad Leadership Presentation:** Pitching the refined prototype to Yulie and Chandu [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc).
* **October 2026:** Target cutoff for the initial M1 release milestones [📄 New Drive Hackathon Goals & Timeline](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1G4Tb3TpuTqwA2VmjO0D_MkD4o6cOdVtCycbSJ9BAlZc).

---
